herbs.herb_desc: Achillea millefolium, also known as yarrow, has been used for centuries to create a natural poultice for wound healing and inflammation. The poultice is typically made by mixing crushed yarrow leaves and flowers with a carrier oil or water, then applying it directly to the affected area to promote healing and reduce pain. The antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties of yarrow make it a popular choice for treating minor cuts, scrapes, and burns, and can also be used to soothe insect bites and stings.
